mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is compatible with the Kafka API. It is designed to be faster and simpler to operate, eliminating the need for ZooKeeper and JVMs. Use Redpanda when you need a lightweight, efficient solution for real-time data streaming.

steps.title: Add Redpanda Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Redpanda Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Redpanda using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

```

> This command adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Redpanda to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Redpanda.

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Redpanda in the specified namespace using the Helm chart. It sets the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

> This command installs Redpanda in the 'redpanda' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for Redpanda, defining the desired state of your Redpanda cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This command creates a Redpanda cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running and ready for use.

```bash

kubectl get redpanda -n redpanda

```

> This command lists the Redpanda resources and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the Redpanda cluster.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

> This command uninstalls the Redpanda release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades Redpanda to the specified version and verifies the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl get redpanda -n redpanda

```

> This command drains the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades Redpanda to the specified version.
> This command verifies that the Redpanda cluster is running the new version.
commands: kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda pods are not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the pods with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce the resource requests in the Redpanda configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redpanda pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redpanda' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to find the Redpanda CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to install the CRDs: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance metrics, added support for new Kafka protocol features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get redpanda -n redpanda' showing your Redpanda cluster with the status 'Ready'.

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
